definition: The US is assessed to be at the 1965-68 'pre-ignition' position in the fiscal dominance transition sequence, with ~7.5/10 similarity score. The five-stage framework places the US between stages (ii) inflation persistence and (iii) bond tantrum, with one tantrum occurred (Oct 2023) followed by partial re-complacification.
evidence: Full-employment deficits, constrained Fed, sticky inflation above target, market complacency, political incapacity to consolidate
Sequence match: fiscal expansion established, inflation sticky, one bond tantrum (Oct 2023), partial re-complacification
Pair_0 debate confirms as strongest transition analogue
Critical caveat: debt/GDP starting point 3x higher (125% vs 40%)
open_questions: Does 3x higher debt/GDP starting point compress or extend transition timeline?
What specific event would mark transition from stage iii to stage iv?
Is the Japan outlier (25+ years quiet period) applicable to US conditions?
What is the analytically correct start date for the current fiscal dominance dynamic?
